nodes.name: Claim Init Trigger
nodes.notes: Initial trigger. Receives: { user_id, policy_number, language }. Returns benefit_type_selector widget.

nodes.name: Respond — Benefit Selector
nodes.notes: Responds to the initial POST with the benefit_type_selector JSON. Execution continues to Wait node.

nodes.name: Wait — Benefit Type
nodes.notes: Pauses execution. Frontend POSTs { benefit_type: 'Dental' } to resumeUrl (webhook-waiting/<exec-id>) to resume.

nodes.name: Map Benefit to Docs
nodes.notes: Receives benefit_type from Wait node body. Maps to required_docs array using claims logic. resumeUrl is the SAME webhook-waiting URL.

nodes.name: Respond — Doc Upload
nodes.notes: Responds to the benefit-type POST with the document_upload widget JSON.

nodes.name: Wait — Doc Upload
nodes.notes: Pauses execution. Frontend POSTs multipart/form-data with binary files to resumeUrl. Each file arrives as a binary property on the item (document_0, document_1, etc).

nodes.name: Transform OCR to Form
nodes.notes: Transforms the OCR service response (status + execution_result shape) into the extracted_form widget format expected by the frontend.

nodes.name: Wait — Form Confirm
nodes.notes: Stage 2: Frontend POSTs form data (claimant_name, provider_name, ..., is_user_edited) to resumeUrl.

nodes.name: Wait — IBAN
nodes.notes: Stage 3: Frontend POSTs { iban, iban_action } to resumeUrl.

nodes.name: Wait — Final Submit
nodes.notes: Stage 4: Frontend POSTs { submit_action, submission_timestamp } to resumeUrl.

options.systemMessage: You are a helpful assistant that is nice to the user. tell him if he needs anything

You are a helpful assistant that understands user's intent.

If the user wants to chat and converse normally, return 'CHAT',if the user wants to submit a claim or deal with claims, return 'CLAIM'. Do not return anything else.
